translations.source: I-UEA neWHO ihambise iziphakamisi moya zamasaplayi ezokwelapha ezibucayi eSudan United Arab Emirates (UAE) neNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni (WHO) bahambise amathoni angu-30 amasaplayi ezokwelapha okuphuthumayo eSudan namhlanje. Ibhanoyi eliphethe amasaplayi yokwelapha abalimele, ukuhlinza okuphuthumayokanye nezidakamizwa eziphuthumayo lifike isikhumulweni samabhanoyi iPort Sudan ekuseni kakhulu. Ukuhanjswa kwenani elingu US$ 444 000, eyokuqala iWHO ekwazile ukuyihambisa ngezomoya iya eSudan kusukela kube nokubheduka kwengxabano. I-WHO ihambise amasaplayi ezindaweni zempilo ngaphambi kokunyuka kwengxabano. Babekhathele ngemva kwezinsuku ezimbalwa uma ubheka inamba yabalimele. Ukukhishwa kwendizamshini kuza njengengxenye ye-UAE imizamo yokuqhubeka nokukhulula ekwesekeni abantu baseSudanese nokuveza ubuhlobo obunzulu neSudan, ikakhulukazi ngokwesimo samanje esibhekene nezwe. Ukukhululwa kuphinde kube ukululwa kobuntu be-UAE umbono nesikalo sobudlelwano namanye amazwe ancike kubantu nouzibophezela kokusiza abanye ngezikhathi zezinkinga nezimo eziphuthumayo. UDkt Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Unqongqoshe wezwe leInternational Cooperation, uthe i-UAE izoqhubeka nokusebenzisana neNhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni ukuqinisekisa ukubekwa okuyimpumelelo kokusebenza kwelojistikhi ebucayi nokuthumela ezokwelapha ngokuphuthuma nosizo lokudla ukusiza ukubhekana nenkinga yamanje eSudan. Nokuhambisana nesimemezelo kuzona zonke izinngxenye zengxabano yamanje ukuyeka manje unya nokuqhuba isimo esidala ukuhlupheka okungashiwo kubantu baseSudan, i-UAE isheshile ekuzibophezelei kwayo ukunikeza usizo nokusiza mazwe ngesikhathi beludinga.
translations.reference: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.hypothesis: The UAE and WHO have dispatched 30 emergency medical supplies to Sudan today. The shipment includes essential medicines, medical equipment, and supplies for treating trauma and other urgent conditions. The delivery, worth US$444,000, is the first major aid shipment from the WHO to Sudan since the outbreak of conflict. The WHO has been working to provide medical assistance to Sudan before the escalation of the conflict. The shipment arrived several days after the WHO began monitoring the situation. The UAE's efforts to provide aid to Sudan are part of its ongoing commitment to supporting the Sudanese people and promoting regional stability, particularly in light of the current crisis. The aid also reflects the UAE's commitment to humanitarian principles and its willingness to work with other countries to provide assistance to those in need. Dr. Reem Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of International Cooperation, said that the UAE will continue to work with the WHO to ensure the effective delivery of emergency medical supplies and other forms of assistance to Sudan. "We are committed to providing the Sudanese people with the support they need during this difficult time," she said.

translations.source: I-UAE igxile kakhulu ekunikezeni amaqoqo abuthaka kakhulu akhahlamezekile kulesisimo saseSudan, ikakhulukazi abagulayo, izingane,nabadala nabesifazane abasengcupheni yokuqhubeka kwengxabano - futhi lamabhanoyi akhululayo azobhekana ngqo nezikhala eziphuthumayo kwezokulapha kanye nokuhlinzekwa ngokudla. I-UAE igcine ngokujula ubuntu kusho ukuthi isiqinisekisile iyaqhubeka nokuxhumana nokumhlaba okukhulu nokuzinikela kokuqinisa ukuthula, ukuphepha nozinzo, ayi esizindeni kuphela kodwa emhlabeni wonke ngesikhathi nababambisani nomphakathi wamazwe angaphandle, izwe liyaqhubeka nokusiza iSudanese ezikhathini ezinzima. Amasaplayi ezempiloafaka ukuthuka okwanele, amasaplayi okuhlinza okuphuthumayo nemithi ebalulekile ukuthi ifike manje kubantu abangu-165 000 abadinga kakhulu usizo lobuntu. Izindawo zezempilo ezweni lonke zibika ukuthi seziphelelwe masaplayi ayisisekelo sezempilo nokuthi izitolo zokwelapha zezwe akusafinyeleki kuzona ngenxa yezokuphepha ezweni. Kokunye ukwesekwa, iWHO izobeka amalojistikhi amabili okuphuthumayo ukuhamba ngebhanoyi ukuqinisekisa lamasaplayi asehanjiswa ezindaweni zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 ukweseka abasebenzi bezempilo nokwengeza ukunakekela kulabo abakudingayo. WHO inamathoni amakhulu angu-30 amasaplayi amalalwveva nezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo njengehayihayi noshukela, izimo ezingabulala uma zishiywa zingalashiwe. Lokhu nezikhwama zegazi ezingu-23 000 sezilungisiwe nelojistikhi yomhlaba yeWHO uhabhu kuInternational Humanitarian City neWHO njengamanje ibheka zonke izindlela zokuletha lamasaplayi eSudan ngokushesha ekubambisaneni noNgqongqoshe weZempilo eSudan. UDkt Michael Ryan, uMqondisi omkhulu wohlelo lweziphuthumayo zempilo weWHO, uthe Iwho Global Logistic Hub e-UAE isibe okubalulekile kokuphindeka kwempendulo yesaplayi esigamekweni ezibucayi emhlabeni. Ukweseka amazwe angaphezu kuka-140 kuzona zonke izindawo zezifunda zweWHO, ihabhu inikezela ngempilo yonke yeMember States kusipiliyoni sokuphuthumayo kwezempilo okuvelayo ekubhedukeni kwezifo, izinhlekelele njengezikhukhula nokuzamazama komhlaba, nomthelela wengxabano ezinhlelweni zempilo. Ukwesekwa okunikeziwe yi-UAE ivumele iWHO ukuthi ibe phambili ekuphenduleni kweziphuthumayo zempilo-ukuhambisa usizo longoti bezokwelapha kulabo abadingayo lapho kudingeka khona.
translations.reference: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.hypothesis: The UAE is primarily focused on providing essential humanitarian aid to the Sudanese people, particularly those in need, including refugees, internally displaced persons, and vulnerable women and children affected by the ongoing conflict. The UAE is committed to ensuring that aid reaches those who need it most, even in the most difficult and dangerous areas. This commitment is reflected in the UAE's ongoing efforts to support Sudanese humanitarian organizations and to work with international partners to provide assistance to those affected by the crisis. The UAE has also provided a significant amount of medical supplies, including vaccines, medicines, and personal protective equipment, to help address the urgent health needs of the Sudanese population. These supplies have been distributed through a network of local and international organizations, and have helped to save lives and prevent the spread of disease. In addition, the UAE has provided financial assistance to support the work of humanitarian organizations in Sudan, and has also provided technical assistance to help them improve their operations. The UAE is also working to support the development of Sudan's health system, and has provided funding for a number of health projects, including the construction of new hospitals and clinics.

translations.source: Njenge ngxenye yokuqinisa ezokwakha umhlaba kwezokulungiselela eziphuthumayo zempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela Iuae, iWHO nokubambisana bayasebenzisana ukuhambisa amasaplayi ukufinyelela kwababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathini sabo abadinga usizo ngayo. Ngengxenye yakhe, uDkt Ahmed Al-Mandhari, uMqondisi wesifunda seWHO wase-Eastern Mediterranean, uthe iNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni izoqhubeka nokuxhumanisa nabasemagunyeni bezempilo eSudan ukuletha amasaplayi kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathi sokudinga usizo kakhulu. Ngenxa yokuxabana, izibhedlela, amakhemisi nezindawo zokunakekela ezempilo yokuqala seziphelelwe okubalulekile, okuyimithi yokusindisa izimpilo. Ukufika kwamathani amakhulu angeziwe awu-30 okukhathazeka namasaplayi ezokuhlinza okuphuthumayo kanye nemithi ebalulekile ukukwazi iWHO izokweseka izindawo zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 nokwenza kabusha kwezempilo umsebenzi wokunakekela kulabo abadingayo. Ilojistikhi yomhhlaba wethu kuhabhu yaseDubai idlala indawo ebaluleke kakhulu ukuqinisekisa ukungaphazamiseki kwesaplayi kwezempilo futhi siyajabula ukwesekwa esikunikiwe yi-United Arab Emirates ukusiza ishatha yokuqala bebhanoyi lalamasaplayi adingeka ngokushesha. Kungukweseka okunjalo njengoba lokhu kukhombisa ukwenza kombono wethu eSifundeni wezempilo kuwona wonke umuntu yiwona wonke umuntu. Usizo lwezokwelapha olunikezwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni nge-UAE kubalelwe kwafinyelela ku-55% isamba samasaplayi okwelapha anikezwe iSudan asuka phesheya ngo-2022, ukuveza isikhundla seUAE njengehabhu enkulu yosizo loluntu emazweni angaphandle.
translations.reference: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies. It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.hypothesis: As part of efforts to strengthen global health preparedness for emergencies, the WHO, along with other international organizations, is working to deliver essential medical supplies to the most vulnerable populations around the world. Dr. Ahmed Al-Mandhari, Regional Director for the WHO's Eastern Mediterranean office, stated that the organization will continue to collaborate with health authorities in Sudan to ensure that essential medical supplies reach those who need them most. Due to the ongoing conflict, healthcare facilities and essential services have been severely disrupted, making it difficult for people to access the care they need. The arrival of these much-needed supplies will help to alleviate the suffering of those affected by the crisis. The global supply chain, which is based in Dubai, plays a crucial role in ensuring that medical supplies reach those who need them quickly. We are grateful for the support of the United Arab Emirates, which has provided us with essential assistance in delivering these vital supplies to Sudan. This is just one example of how our commitment to global health is making a difference in the lives of people around the world.

translations.source: Ukubambisana endaweni kubalulekile ekuqhubekeni emhlabeni okuya kokuhlobene nezempilo zamaSDG Umbiko wokuqhubeka omusha ukhishwe ngokuhlanganyela namhlanje kuma ejensi asayinile angu-13 eGlobal Action Plan for Helath Lives and Well being for All (SDG3 GAP). Umbiko usihloko esthi "What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?" uma kubhekwa iminyaka emine kusetshenzwa ndawonye ngama-ejensi ezindlela eziningi ezinkulu kuvezwa ukuthi yikuphi okusebenzile nokungasebenzanga ekuqinisweni ukubambisana nokwesekwa kwenqubeko yezwe okuuya nokuhlobene nezempilo zeSustainable Development Goals (amaSDG). Lo nyaka umaka iphuzu lahhafu eliya kumaSDG kodwa umhlaba usele ngemuva ukufika kuzinhloso zomhlaba. Kusukela ngokwethulwa kwayo ngo-2019, iSDG3 GAP yenze ukubambisana okusha kwezinhlaka phakathi kokusayina kwama ejensi ezindaweni ezibalulekile njengokugcineka kwezimali nokunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala kwabanye abantu. Okungenani amazwe angu-67 akhulumile kanye noma ngaphezulu ngeSDG3 GAP ukuqhuba izingqikithi. Senze inqubeko ebalulekile, kodwa kusanendlela ende okumele siyihambe ukwenza ngcono indlela izinhlangano eziningi ezisebenzisana ngayo ukweseka amazwe. Kumele silalele esikutshelwa ngamazwe futhi silandele imiyalelo,kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO noSihlalo weSDG3 GAP kuzinhloko zeqoqo. "Ngibonga ababambisani ngokubambisana kwazo nokuzihlola ngokwethembeka okuqukethwe kulombiko. "Umbiko unikeza iziphakamiso eziyisithupha ezibalulekile ukuqinisekisa ukuthi amazwe ayazuza ekwesekweni okukhishiwe nokubona ukuzibophezela okwenziwe kuSDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis: Collaboration is crucial for achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). A new progress report was released today through a partnership between the 13 organizations that signed the Global Action Plan for Health Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report addresses the question "What worked? What didn't? What's next?" after four years of collaborative work by these organizations. The report highlights the successes and challenges of collaborative efforts to achieve SDG3, as well as the importance of continued partnership and support. This year marks the halfway point of the SDG3 GAP, but the world is still far from achieving its goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP has fostered new partnerships between different sectors, including finance, health, and education. Sixty-seven countries have now signed up to the SDG3 GAP, demonstrating its growing importance. "We need to learn from each other and build on our successes," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO and Chair of the SDG3 GAP. "I thank all the partners for their hard work and dedication." The report provides valuable insights and recommendations to help countries achieve their SDG3 targets.

translations.source: Lokhu kufaka: Ukuqhubeka kokwenza ngcono kweSDG3 GAP kwezempilo ngokufuna imibono yeMember State ukuthi sibambisana kanjani ezingeni lezwe nokuphendula okuhlobene neziphakamiso; Ukugcina iGAP njengenkundla yokubambisana; ukulondoloza ukubambisana okuqinile ezingeni lezwe ekunakekelweni kwezempilo kokuqala nokubheka izindlela ezintsha njengesimo sezulu sokubekezela kuzinhlelo zempilo; Ngokuhlanganyela sebenzisa izindlela ezintsha ezingeni lezwe njengokuhambisa indlela yomthelela; ukukhuluma kakhulu nomphakathi; kanye nokusebenza neMember States ukuqinisa imiklomelo yokubambisana ngobuholi bezepolitiki,indlela yokubusa noxhasomali lokweseka ukubambisana. Imiphumela ebalulekile yombiko sizoyifaka ezingxoxweni kuUnited Nations General Assembly SDG Summit elandelayo nomhlangano wezinga eliphezulu kuUniversal Helath Coverage ngoMandulo 2023. Izinyanga eziholela kulezigameko ezibalulekile zinikeza ithuba ukuphusha ndawonye yiMember States kamaejensi ezindlela eziningi ukuhlonza izindlela ukuthi kuzokwenziwa kanjani iziphakamiso ukulungiselella indawo yesibambiso esiphelele ukuqina kokubambisana nokuqhuba inqubeko eya kokuhlobene nezempilo SDG kuhhafu wesibili weSDG wesikhathi esibekiwe. Inothi eliya kumhleli:
translations.reference: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.hypothesis: This includes: Continuing to improve the implementation of SDG3 GAP for health by seeking input from Member States on how to best collaborate at the national level and respond to recommendations; Maintaining the GAP as a platform for collaboration; ensuring strong collaboration at the national level in the provision of primary health care and exploring new approaches such as a national health insurance scheme; Promoting the use of innovative approaches at the national level, such as mobile health services; engaging with communities; and working with Member States to strengthen political commitment to collaboration through policy, governance, and funding mechanisms. Key outcomes of the meeting will be presented at the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the Universal Health Coverage High-Level Meeting in 2023. The preparatory work for these important meetings provides an opportunity for Member States to share best practices and develop concrete recommendations for achieving a fully functional and sustainable collaboration framework to support the implementation of SDG3 and the second half of the SDG 3 timeframe. Note to editor:

translations.source: I-SDG3 GAPichaza ukuthi ukusayina kwama ejensi angu-13 kuzithatha kanjani izindlela ezintsha zokusebenza, ukwakha phezu kokubambisana okukhona okuphumelele, ukubambisana okuhambisana nokweseka kwabo ezinhlelweni zezwe namasu okungawezwe futhi aholwa yilo. Ukusayidwa kuSDG3 GAP kuyiGavi ubudlelwane bomgomo; Indawo yezezimali zomhlaba yabesifazane izingane nenstha (GFF); uxhasomali lomhlaba lokulwa kengculaza,isifo sofuba nomalaleveva(Global Fund); I-International Labour Organisation (ILO); Uhlelo lokuhlanganyela lweUnited Nations lwe HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); I-United Nation Development Fund (UNDP); I-United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); I-UnitedNations Children Fund(UNICEF); Unitaid; I-United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.reference: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.hypothesis: SDG3 GAP highlights how the 13 targets of this Sustainable Development Goal are being addressed through innovative approaches, building on existing successful partnerships, and fostering collaboration across different sectors and levels of government. The SDG3 GAP encompasses a range of partnerships: the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria; the Global Fund for Women, Girls and Adolescents; the Global Fund for the Fight Against AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria; the International Labour Organisation (ILO); the United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); the United Nations Development Fund (UNDP); the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF); UNAIDS; UN Women;

translations.source: Imiphumela yocwaningo olusha ikhombisa izinhlelo zempilo ziqala ukuba ngcono esikhathini sobhubhane Ngemva kweminyaka emithathu yobhubhane lweCOVID-19 izinhlelo zempilo emazweni seziqale ukukhombisa izinkomba ezinkulu zokuvuseleleka kwezinhlelo zempilo, ngokweWHO umbiko wesikhashana emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lephalisi yomhlaba ekuqhubekeni kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19: Lwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ngokuqala kuka-2023 amazwe abike ukwenzeka kokwehla kokuphazamiseka ekulethweni kwendlela yemisebenzi yezempilo,kodwa kwavezwa isidingo sokutshala kokungalungiswa nokubekezela kakhulu kwikusasa. Kulamazwe angu-139 aphendule kumzuliswane wesine wokuzwakala kocwaningo lweWHO, amazwe abike ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka emisebenzini yekota esilinganisweni. Emazweni angu-84 lapho ukuhlaziya okudumile kungenzeka,iphesenti lemisebenzi ephazamisekile yehlile ngesilinganiso esisuka ku-56% ngoNtulikazi kuya kuMandulo 2020 ngo-23 % ngoLwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka kungenxa yakho kokubili ukufuneka nokusaplaya izinkinga eziseceleni, okufaka amazinga aphansi okunakekelwa kwezempilo emphakathini okudingayo kanye nomkhawulo wokubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo nezinsizakusebenza ezinye zokunakekelwa kwezempilo njengokuvulwa kwemitholampilo noma ukubakhona kwesitoko semithi nemikhiqizo. "Izindaba ezamukelwayo ukuthi izinhlelozempilo emzaweni amaningi ziqala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ezigidini zabantu abawugejile ngesikhathi sobhubhane," kusho uDkt Rudi Eggers, Umqondisi weWHO weMisebenzi yeZempilo yoKuhlanganisiwe. "Kodwa sidinga ukuqinisekisa ukuthi wonke amazwe ayaqhubeka nokuvala izikhala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo,nokusebenzisa izifundo abazifundile ukwakha izinhlelo zempilo ezizilungiselele nokubekezela kwekusasa". Izimpawu zokuqala eziqaphelekayo ekuvuseleleni Kulolu cwaningo olusha,amazwe ambalwa abike ngokungenhloso isikalo sasemuva sokuthola kuzinkundla zonke umsebenzi wokuletha nokusebenza kwezempilo yomphakathi okubalulekile kusukela 2020-2021 ukubikwa kukhombisa ukubaluleka kwegxathu kubuyelwe emazingeni angaphambi kobhubhane ukulethwa kokusebenza nokusebenza okukhulu kwezinhlelo.
translations.reference: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.hypothesis: The findings of the new study show that healthcare systems are starting to recover after three years of the COVID-19 pandemic. According to a WHO interim report released in the fourth round of the global health security agenda, healthcare systems in many countries have shown significant signs of recovery, including increased capacity to provide essential healthcare services during the COVID-19 pandemic: 2022-2023. In the first half of 2023, many countries reported a decrease in disruptions to essential healthcare services, but there is still a need for continued investment and planning for the future. In 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of the WHO's global health security agenda, disruptions to essential healthcare services remained a concern. In 84 countries where disruptions were possible, the percentage of essential healthcare services disrupted decreased from an average of 56% in the first half of 2020 to 23% in the first half of 2022-2023. The continued disruptions are due to a combination of factors, including the ongoing need to address underlying health challenges, as well as the impact of the pandemic on healthcare workers and other essential healthcare providers. "It is encouraging to see that healthcare systems in many countries are starting to recover from the disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic," said Dr. Rudi Eggers, WHO Director of Global Health Security. "However, it is important to ensure that all countries continue to invest in strengthening their healthcare systems and to use the lessons learned from the pandemic to build more resilient and sustainable healthcare systems for the future." Some of the key findings from the new study include:

translations.source: Ekupheleni kuka-2022, amazwe amaningi abike izimpawu ezingacacile zokuvuselelwa kokusebenza okufaka ukusebenza kwezocansi, ukuzala, abakhullwe, abazelwe, izingane nezempilo yentsha, ukudla okunempilo ukugomma,izifo ezithelelanayo (okufaka umalaleveva, iHIV, iTB nezinye izifo ezithelelana ngokocansi) izifo ezidumile ezinganakiwe,izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo,ukuphathwa kwengqondo, okwengqondo nesifo sokusebenzisa izidakamiza, ukunakekelwa kwabantu abadala nokunakekelwa okulekelelayo nokwesiko. Inombolo yamazwe abika ukuphazamiseka ohlelweni lwawo lesaplayi sheni ezweni okungafika kuhhafu (29 ka-59 wamazwe aphendulayo) okunganekota (18 wa-66 wamazwe) aphedulayo kulonyaka odlule. Ngaphandle kwezinkomba zokuvuselela,ukuphazamiseka komsebenzi kuyaqhubeka emazweni onke namazinga emali engenayo kuzona zonke izinhlelo soomsebenzi wokulatha nokulandelela umsebenzi ezindaweni. Amazwe aphinde abhekane nokunyuka komsebenzi okumele wenziwe - ikakhulukazi emisebenzini yokubheka, ukuxilonga nokwelapha izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo - okungaholela kumiphumela engemihle uma abantu bebambezeleka ukuthola ukunakekelwa ngesikhathi. Ukuvuselelwa kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile kubucayi ngenxa yokuphazamiseka - okufaka imisebenzi yokugqugquzela ezempilo, ukuvinjwa kwezifo, ukwelapha, ukuvuselelwa kwesimilo nephaliyeshini - kungaba nokukhulu umthelela ohlukene wezempil nezinga lomuntu ukunobhubhane qobo ikakhulukazi kubantu babuthaka. Ukuhlanganisa imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 emisebenzini yezempilo ebalulekile elinye igxathu elibalulekile eliya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo noguquko,amazwe amaningi asebe nenqubekela phambili ekuhlanganiseni imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 endleleni yokuletha imisebenzi yezempilo. Cishe u-80%-90% wamazwe ahlanganyele ngokuphelele ekugonyweni kweCOVID-19 ukuxilonga nemisebenzi yokuphathwa kwecala kanye nemisebenzi yesimo sangemuva kweCOVID-19 endleleni yomsebenzi wokuletha. Kuyafana amazwe amaningi (u-80% wamazwe awu-83 aphendulile) abike okungenani okuncane ekukhuphukeni kokuthola amathuluzi abalulekile eCovid-19 (isibonelo ukuxilongwa kweCOVID-19, ukwelaphangxube, imigomo namathuluzi okuzivikela siqu - PPE), ngezinkinga zokusebenza kwezempilo nokwentuleka koxhasomali okumele izivimbo ezijwayeleke kakhulu. Ukuqhubeka kokusekwa kuyadingeka ukuvuselela,ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela Amazwe amaningi aseqalile asebenzisa asebekufundile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19 okufaka ngezikhungo zenamba yokusbenza kokusha ukuphazamisa amasu endleleni yokulethwa kwezempilo.
translations.reference: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.hypothesis: At the end of 2022, many countries reported clear signs of a significant disruption to essential health services, including maternal and child health, immunization, mental health, and the treatment of non-communicable diseases (such as cardiovascular disease, HIV, tuberculosis, and cancer), as well as the management of mental health conditions, substance use disorders, and palliative care. Approximately half (29-59%) of countries surveyed reported a decline in the availability of essential health services, while 18-66% reported a decline in the quality of these services in the previous year. In addition to the disruption to essential health services, there was also a continued increase in the burden of work in low- and middle-income countries, particularly in the areas of primary care, disease surveillance and control, and the management of non-communicable diseases. This increased workload can lead to burnout and reduced quality of care, particularly for healthcare workers. The disruption to essential health services is also likely to have a disproportionate impact on vulnerable populations, such as those living in rural areas or those with limited access to healthcare. The integration of COVID-19-related activities into essential health services is another important factor to consider when assessing the impact of the pandemic on health systems. Many countries have successfully integrated COVID-19 testing, vaccination, and treatment into their existing health services. Approximately 80-90% of countries surveyed have fully integrated these activities into their health systems. However, there are still challenges to overcome, such as ensuring that healthcare workers have the necessary training and resources to deliver these services, and addressing the potential for increased workload and burnout. Continued investment in essential health services is essential to mitigate the impact of the pandemic and ensure that all people have access to the healthcare they need.

translations.source: Lokhu kufaka ukubekwa kwezindlela zokwelapha ngefoni,ukugqugquzela ukunakekelwa usekhaya nokungenelela kokuzinakekela, izindlela zokuqinisa ukubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo, amandla nezindlela zokweseka, ukwenza kabusha ekuthengeni nokuletha imithi namasaplayi ezinye izindlela zokuxhumana nomphakathi ukubambisana nabanikezeli bomkhakha ozimele nowomphakathi. Amakota amathathu ezwe abaike ukufakwa koxhasomali olwengeziwe oluya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo lwesikhathi eside, ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela. Amazwe agcizelele isidingo sokwesekwa kweWHO ekubhekaneni nezinkinga ezisele ngokwengqikithi yeCOVID-19 nangale,ikakhulukazi okujwayele ukuhlobana nokuqinisa ukusebenza kwezempilo, ukwakha amandla okubheka imisebenzi yezempilo, ukwakha ukunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala okuncike kuzindlela zokunakekela, ukubusa, inqubomgomo nokuhlela nokuhlela ezezimali noxhasomali. Inothi kubahleli: Emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lwephasile yomhlaba weWHO, amazwe angu-222, izindawo bamenywa ukuba baphendule kucwaningo oluncike kuwebhu eqinisikesiwe phakathi kukaLwezi 2022 noMasingana 2023. Ucwaningo lwesine lulandele kucwaningo lwephasile edlule yango-2020 no2021 lweWHO: uMzuliswano 1 (Nhlaba-Mandulo 2020) uMzuliswano 2 (Masingana-Ndasa 2021) noMzuliswano 3 (Lwezi-Zibandlela 2021) okwakhombisa ukukhulu bokuthi ubhubhane lalukhahlameza ukuqhubeka kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile nokuthi amazwe enza kanjani. Ngesikhathi ucwaningo lwephasile lunemikhawulo njengokubika ukuchema nokumela,amandla alomzamo ukuthi mukhulu futhi uletha ulwazi ngokuphindekile. Itemu "izwe" kumele liqondwe ukuthi lifaka wonke amazwe, imingcele nezindawo. Abahlaziya okudumile kwaqedwa kuncike ezimpendulweni eziphuma emazweni angu-84, abahlukumezi noma izindawo ezaphendula okungenani kusahluko socwaningo olulodwa kuyona yonke imizuliswano emine yocwaningo.
translations.reference: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action. While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.hypothesis: This includes the implementation of telehealth services, promoting home-based care, and strengthening the capacity of healthcare workers, as well as access to essential medicines and other health-related resources. The three countries highlighted the need for increased financial support to sustain long-term health systems, preparedness, and response efforts. The countries emphasized the importance of WHO support in addressing the remaining challenges related to COVID-19, particularly in areas such as strengthening health systems, building workforce capacity, ensuring access to essential health services, and mobilizing financial resources. The findings of the fourth round of the WHO's global survey, conducted between 2022 and 2023, show that 222 countries and territories responded to the survey. The survey followed up on the third round of the WHO's global survey, conducted in 2020 and 2021 (Round 1: 2020, Round 2: 2021, and Round 3: 2021), which highlighted the significant impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on health systems and the measures countries were taking to address the challenges.

translations.source: Isitatimende sokubungaza ukuphela kweminyaka engu-50 yeHRP: iSweden ne-United Kingdom of Great Britain neNorthen Ireland Ukubamba isimo seNqubomgomo neKomidi lokuXhumana (PCC) Amalungu abungaza ukuphela kuka-50 HRP nokuzuza okuningi, iSweden neUnited Kingdom of Great Britain neNorthern Ireland ukufunda ukuqapheleka okulandelayo kokwamukela nokuhalalisa emhlanganweni wesi-36 weHRP PCC. Isizukulwane sethu sesibone inqubekela phambili enkulu kusayensi nezobuchwepheshe ukuthi konke okuqhubekayo kwesizukulwane ndawonye, nokubona okwemfundo okumbalwa,ezobuchwepheshe, ezokwelapha noguquko lwenhlalakahle luqhutshwa ukuguquka kokuvikela kulandelwa ukwenzangcono kwezempilo yokuzala, ukwenza kabusha kwezempilo kanye namalungelo nokulingana ngokobulili. Kepha ukucabanga izingqinamba emkhakheni weSRHR uqhubeke futhi kuphikiswane,okunye ukuqhubeka kusembuzweni futhi namhlanje. Sincoma abasebenzi beHRP ababambisani nabeseki kwabakutholile okuningi kuze kube yinamhlanje nokubona izinto ezinkulu ezenzekile ngesikhathi esinguhhafu wekhulunyaka ekukhiqizeni ubufakazi obeseka ukungena okuphelele nokulinganayo kwezempilo namalungelo ezocansi nokuzala. Singathanda ukuveza imikhakha emikhulu emithathu yokuzuziwe Okokuqala ucwaningo olusezingeni eliphezulu lweHRP lube nomthelela obonakalayo kunqubomgomo yezempilo yomphakathi,amazinga, nokwenza emhlabeni wonke okubhekene nezidingo zokuguquka kusibalo sabantu esahlukene. Okwesibili HRP iqapheleke ngeHRP Alliance, isiqinise izikhungo zocwaningpo endaweni nasemhlabeni,kwavumela abacwaningi abaningi ukuthi bathole amakhono okucwabinga ukuqinisekisa ubunhalo obuphezulu bocwaningo lweSRHR ukuthi liqhubeke futhi lunyuke esikhathini esizayo. Okwesithathu,umhlaba uhlelwa yizinkinga, ukuxabana nokunyusa ukuqaba ekulinganeni kobulili, iHRP isize ukubamba isisekelo samalungelo abasefazane nezingane nabantu ababuthaka. I-HRP ihlala iyithuluzi elibalulekile kuhlelo lweUnited Nations yocwaningo lokhukhiqiza kubantu nokuqhubeka nokuba umholi ovelele kwezempilo yezocansi nokuzala emhlabeni.
translations.reference: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.hypothesis: Statement on the 50th Anniversary of HRP: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland Addressing the status of the Human Rights Programme (HRP) and the Committee on Human Rights (PCC) Members of the HRP and other stakeholders, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, will participate in the 36th HRP PCC meeting. Our generation is witnessing a significant shift in science and technology, with advancements in education, technology, healthcare, and social development transforming the way we live. However, challenges remain in the area of SRHR, and progress is not always linear. We advocate for continued investment in HRP and for the development of innovative solutions to address the complex issues facing women and girls today. We would like to highlight three key areas for future HRP focus: First, research on the impact of SRHR on the health and well-being of women and girls. Second, the development of new technologies and interventions to address SRHR challenges. Third, the promotion of SRHR education and awareness among young people.

translations.source: HRP nababambisani bayo sebesisondeze kusithembiso sango-1994 KuNkomfa Yamazwe angaphandle Esibalweni Sabantu neNtuthuko, futhi isimemezelo sibeka amalungelo abantu ikakhulukazi abesifazane namantombazane phakathi nokugcineka kwentuthuko. Sifisa ukuzwakalisa ukubonga nokujabula kwethu kunobhala weHRP, ababambisani, kanye nabaxhasi kanye nohlelokuxhumana lomhlaba ezizindeni zokubambisana nongoti abasiza ukwenza isiqiniseko ukuthi imizamo inikela ekwenzeni umehluko kubantu besifazane, amadoda, izingane, intsha, nemiphakathini eyahlukene iHRP izimisele ngokusiza. Ngokubambela amalunga ePCC sivumeleni siqinisekise ukubambisana kwethu nani ukweseka okuzuziwe kweHRP ekuphumeleleni okuncike ku-ajenda 2030.
translations.reference: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.hypothesis: The HRP and its partners have been instrumental in promoting the principles of the 1994 Beijing Declaration and Platform of Action, which emphasize the rights of women and girls in the context of development. We would like to express our gratitude and appreciation to the HRP secretariat, its partners, and the global network of civil society organizations that have worked to ensure that the efforts of the HRP contribute to achieving the goals of the 2030 Agenda.

translations.source: I-WHO yethula umzamo omusha wokwenza ngcono ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane Ukusiza amazwe ukuthi azilungisele okungcono kwekusasa lobhubhane,WHO yethule umzamo namahlanje ozonikeza imiyalelo yokuhlela ngokuhlanganisa ngokuphendula kunoma iyiphi iphathojini yokuphefumula njengomkhuhlane noma ikhorona. I-Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, noma iPRET, ihlanganisa amathuluzi akamuva nezindlela zokufunda okwabelwana ngazo kanye nesenzo esiyiqoqo esisungulwe phakathi nobhadane lweCOVID-19 nezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zakamuva zezempilo zomphakathi. Ngomzamo iWHO izosebenzisa indlela yokudlulisela ukuyala amazwe ekuhleleni ubhubhane,ngoba kukhona amandla amaningi namandla amaningi afanayo kumaqoqo ephathojini. I-PRET iphendula isimemezelo somuyalelo wezobuchwepheshe nokwesekwa kokugqugquzelwa nokuqiniswa kokuhlanganisa ukuzilungiselela nokuphendula,njengova kuveziwe kuzinqumo zeWorld Health Assembly. Ububhane lweCOVID-19 nezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zezempilo zikhombisile ukuthi amazwe adinga ukuthi alungele ukubhekana nezinsongo zezifo ezithathelwanayo, ngezinhlelo zokuzilungiselela ezilungiselelwe ngesandla kanye nokuxhumana okungcono nokusebenzisana neminye imikhakha efana nezolimo. "Ukuzilungiselela, ukuvikela nemisebenzi yokuphendula akumele kube yisifundazwe somkhakha wezempilo kuphela," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Njengeziphuthumayo zempilo zinomthelela kumikhakha eminingi, ngakho nokuzilungiselela kwethu nemizamo yokuphendula mayilingane nemikhakah, izinhlobo namagciwane. Kubucayi futhi ukuthi ukukhuluma komphakathi nokulingana kuphakathi kwemizamo yethu, ikakhulukazi kulabo bantu abagcindezelekile futhi abasengcupheni enkulu." I-PRET iwuguquko endleleni yeWHO ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane ngokusebenzisa indlela yelensi yokudlulisela, ukunokugxila ezifweni ezithile.
translations.reference: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.hypothesis:
WHO has launched a new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness. The initiative, called the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative (PRET), aims to provide countries with the tools and resources they need to prepare for a range of emerging health threats, including pandemics. The initiative will also focus on building capacity and strengthening coordination among different sectors, such as health, agriculture, and finance.

"Pandemics are a global challenge that requires a coordinated response," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. "By working together, we can better prepare for and respond to future pandemics. The PRET initiative is a key part of our efforts to strengthen global pandemic preparedness."

The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the need for countries to be prepared for a range of health emergencies. The pandemic has also shown the importance of strong public health systems, as well as effective communication and coordination among different sectors.

The PRET initiative is a significant step forward in WHO's efforts to improve pandemic preparedness. By focusing on specific threats and building capacity in key areas, the initiative will help countries to be better prepared for future pandemics.

translations.source: I-WHO izoqhubeka nokuthuthukisa nokusabalalisa imiyalelo ezifweni ezithile njengoba kudingeka. Ukwethulwa komzamo kuzokwenzeka ngosuku lokugcina lwemihlangano abangaphezu kuka-120 abamele Member State, ababambisani, noNobhala weWHO, ukuchaza indlela eya phambili yePRET okufaka ukuthuthuka kwebalazwe lokwenza lomhlaba kokuzilungiselela lobhubhane lwegciwane lokuphefumula. Imizamo yePRET isifundo sokuqala sigxile kumagciwane pokuphefumula, okufaka umkhuhlane, igciwane lekhorona namagciwane ezokuphefumula. Ngokunikezwa ubhubhane oluqhubekayo nokwesabisa okungenzeka emkhuhlaneni, lemojuli izokwenza amazwe abheke ngokucophelela, ukuhlola, ukubuyekeza imizamo yokuhlela ubhubhane lwezokuphefumula ukuqinisekisa banamandla okusebenzayo, namandla akhona. Uhlelo lusendleleni lokuhlonza iqoqo elilandelayo lamagciwane, njengama-arbovirus, ukuthi kubhekwane nawo ngaphansi kwalomzamo. Lokhu kuzolandela okuhamba phambili okuhlonziwe ngeziphakamiso eziyishumi zokuqinisa ukwakhiwa komhlaba kokuzilungiselela kokuphuthuma kwezempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela (HEPR). Umzamo wePRET ukuyisa esikhathini esisha sokuzilungiselela kobhubhane nokumela uguquko lwemisebenzi ngqo yeWHO ukweseka wonke amaMember State ekuqiniseni ezempilo zokuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo, ukuvikela namandla okuphendula nokukwazi. Umzamo ungaphinde usize ukusebenzisa izinjongo nokuhlinzekwa kwePandemic Accord okwamanje okusaxoxwa ngayo yiWHO Member State. Kulandela lokho okufakiwe nezingxoxo emihlanganweni yababambe iqhaza, iWHO imema ababambisani kulesimemezelo somhlaba sokwenza ukuwandisa ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane legciwane lokuphefumula. Kufuneka ukwenza
translations.reference: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.hypothesis: WHO will continue to promote and disseminate guidelines for specific health issues as needed. The launch of this initiative will take place on the final day of the 120+ member state, partner, and WHO Director-General meetings, outlining the way forward for the global effort to prepare for a pandemic of influenza. The PREP initiative focuses on influenza viruses, including avian, swine, and human influenza. By providing ongoing surveillance and data on influenza activity, the initiative will enable countries to make informed decisions, conduct thorough assessments, and update their influenza preparedness plans to ensure they have the resources and capacity to respond effectively. The initiative is also designed to support the surveillance of other emerging pathogens, such as arboviruses, which may pose a threat to human health. This will involve the development of new tools and methods for detecting and monitoring these pathogens. The PREP initiative is part of a broader effort to strengthen global pandemic preparedness and response, and it complements the work of the WHO in ensuring that all member states have the capacity to respond to public health emergencies. The initiative will also help to leverage the existing Pandemic Accord, which was adopted by WHO member states in 2016. Following the meetings, WHO will encourage member states to adopt the global strategy for influenza preparedness and response.

translations.source: AmaMember State eWHO, nababambisani basebenza ndawonye ukuhlonza nokuhambisa phambili ukwenza izenzo zokuqinisa izwe, isifunda, nokuzilungiselela komhlaba kwezobhubhane nokuvela kwezifo ezithelelanayo ezisabisayo. Siyayibona indima yomphakathi nayo yonke imikhakha sidinga ukulinganisa nokugcina umfutho ekupheleni kwesakile yokusaba nokunganaki lokho kujwayelekile ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane nokuphendula. Sikhumbula izifundo zobhubhane olwedlule ne-ephidemikhi, ikakhulukazi lezo ezidalwa amagciwane okuphefumula. Lawo magciwane azoqhubeka nokuletha ukusabisa komhlaba okukhulu ngamandla okudala izinkinga ezinkulu nokufa, izinhlelo zempilo ezingaphezu kwamandla, ukwehlisa ezomnotho zomhlaba, ukwengeza ukungalingani, okukhona ekungeneni kumathuluzi omabili okuvikela ubhubhane nokunakekelwa ezempilo kubona bonke abantu. Lesi yisimemezelo sokwenza ukuqhubeza ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane nokuvela kokwesaba emhlabeni. Ukusebenza kokuzilungiselela kuncike kubukhulu bokuhlela nokwenza kokuxhumana. Ukuzimisela kweqoqo kuzenze ezilandelayo kuzobonisa ukuqhubeka okutholakele ngoZibandlela 2025 futhi kuzochazwa (Ukuzilungiselela nokubekezela kokusabisayo okuvelayo (PRET) ukubheka umhlahlandlela: Ukulungiselelwa kokubuyekeziwe ukuhlela okuqinisekisa izenzo zokuhamba phambili nokucabanga izifundo zezigemegeme ezedlule. Ukubona ingcuphe efakwe amagciwane ezokuphefumula, ukuhlela kobhubhane lamagciwane ezokuphefumula ancike kuzingqikithi ezihlonzwe kusifundo sePRET #1: Ukuhlelela ubhubhane lwamagciwane ezokuphefumula kuhamba phambili
translations.reference: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally. Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.hypothesis: The Member States of the WHO, along with their partners, are working together to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for outbreaks and pandemics. We recognize the critical role of community engagement and multisectoral collaboration in preventing and responding to these threats. We draw on lessons from past outbreaks and pandemics, particularly those caused by respiratory viruses. These viruses continue to pose a significant threat to global health, causing widespread illness, death, and economic disruption. They also exacerbate existing inequalities, making it more difficult for vulnerable populations to access essential healthcare services. This underscores the need for continued investment in pandemic preparedness and response. Preparedness efforts depend on a range of factors, including planning, communication, and coordination. The success of future preparedness efforts will depend on our ability to learn from past experiences and to adapt our strategies accordingly.

translations.source: Ukunyusa ukuxhumana kubabambisani ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane ukuhlela ngezinhlelo zokuxhumana nokusebenzisana. Lokhu kufaka ukwakha izinhlelo ezilinganayo ukwenza imisebenzi ngokuhlanganyela; Nokwabelana ngolwazi ekwenzeni okuhle, izingqinamba kanye namathuba. Ukunikela ngotshalomali olugcinekile, izimali nokubheka kokulungela ubhubhane ngokugxila ekubhekeni izikhala ezihlonze ngesikhathi sobhubhane olwedlule. Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 luphendule ukukhombisa ukuthi yikuphi okungazuzwa ngokuzibophezela kwezepolitiki, ukukhuluma komphakathi kanye noxhasomali. Phakathi nalomsebenzi ukuqinisekisa ukulingana ukulungela obhubhane olulandelayo ndawonye. Ukwenza komphakathi wonke kuyadingeka ukwenza inqubekela phambili evezwe kule simemezelo sokwenza. Ukwenza kumele kuqinise imiphakathi ebekezelayo; egcinayo nokugcineka nokwakha izindlela zezinhlelo; nokubambisa kwamandla amakhulu kokuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo nokuphendula. Lesi simemezelo sokwenza sakhishwa eKuzilungiseleni nokubekezela kokuvela kokusabisayo (PRET): Umhlangano womhlaba wekusasa lobhubhane lwamagciwane ezokuphefumula obanjwe ngo-24-26 Mbasa 2023 eGeneva, eSwitzerland.
translations.reference: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis: Enhancing communication is crucial for effective pandemic preparedness, including planning and coordination. This involves creating clear communication channels to facilitate collaboration; sharing information about best practices, challenges, and opportunities. It also includes providing secure funding, resources, and a focus on identifying and addressing vulnerabilities during the pandemic. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of self-reliance, community engagement, and global cooperation. In addition to these efforts, it is essential to ensure that future pandemic preparedness plans are aligned. Building strong, resilient, and responsive communities is essential for success. This requires investing in public health infrastructure; promoting community engagement; and fostering collaboration between different sectors. This pandemic preparedness plan is based on the principles of the Global Preparedness Initiative (GPI): a global effort to strengthen pandemic preparedness and response. The GPI was launched in 2017 at the World Health Assembly in Geneva, Switzerland.

translations.source: I-WHO inxenxa ukunyuswa kokwenza ngamathuluzi aphakanyisiwe ukulwa nomalaleveva Ukumaka uSuku lukaMalaleveva eMhlabeni, Inhlangano Yezempilo Yomhlaba (WHO) izokhipha isimememzelo sokunyusa ukwenza kokungenelela okukhona nokusha ukusindisa izimpilo kumalaleveva. Okungafika kuzigidi ezingu-1.5 izingane ezisengcupheni enkulu yokugula nokufa okusuka kumalaleveva eGhana, eKenya naseMalawi manje sebethole umgomo wokuqala kamalaleveva, RTS, S/AS01 (RTS,S), siyabonga kuhlelo lokuhlola oluqhubekayo oluxhunyaniswa yiWHO. Ukuhlolwa komgomo wamalaleveva, owethulwe ngo-2019,kwenyusa ukulingana kokuthola ukuvikelwa kumalaleveva kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu futhi kusindisa izimpilo. Ukwenziwe kakhulu, iWHO ilinganisela ukuthi umgomo kamalaleveva ungasingisa izimpilo eziyizinkulungwane eziyishumi zezingane ngonyaka. "Sinamathuluzi ukwehlisa umalaleveva,iphekheji yokungenenela ebandakanya ukulawula ivektha,imithi yokuvikela, ukuhlola nokwelapha," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Lokhu kulandelwa ngumgomo ophephile nosebenzayo kamalaleveva, ongasindisa izimpilo eziyizinkulungwane eziyishumi zezingane unyaka ngamunye. Ngotshalomali olugcinekile nokunyuka kwemizamo ukufika kulabo abasengcupheni kakhulu, ukuqedwa kwamalaleveva emazweni amaningi sekuyafika. "Usuku lukaMalaleveva eMhlabeni 2023 lumakwe ngaphansi kwengqikithi, "Isikhathi sokuwqeda umalaleveva, tshala, yenza kabusha yenza". Kule ngqikithi, iWHO inxenxa okunye ukwenza okusebenzayo kwamathuluzi akhona namasu okuvikela, ukuxilonga nokwelapha umalaleveva, ikakhulukazi kulesosibalo esingcindezelekile.
translations.reference: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO is investigating the use of innovative tools to combat malaria. The World Health Organization (WHO) will launch a campaign to promote the use of existing and new tools to prevent and treat malaria. This could save the lives of millions of children in sub-Saharan Africa, where malaria is a major cause of illness and death. The RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S) vaccine, developed in partnership with the WHO, is the first malaria vaccine to be recommended by the organization. The vaccine has been shown to be safe and effective in clinical trials, and it is now being rolled out in several African countries. The WHO estimates that the vaccine could prevent hundreds of thousands of malaria cases each year. "We have the tools to defeat malaria," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO. "We need to ensure that these tools are used effectively and that everyone has access to them." The WHO is also working to develop new and more effective malaria control tools, including new vaccines, insecticides, and diagnostic tests.

translations.source: Ngokombiko wokugcina wamalaleveva emhlabeni,oshicilelwe ngoZibandlela 2022, bekunesilinganiso sika-247 wezigidi zamacala amasha kamalaleveva ngo-2021. I-WHO African Region iyaqhubeka ukuthwala umthwalo onzima wesifo- okuballelwa esilinganiswenisa ku-95% wamacala kamalaleveva (u-234 wezigidi) no-96% wokufa (593 000) ngo-2021. Kungafika ku-80% ukufa okudalwa umalaleveva eSifundeni se-Afrika obukukubantwana abangaphansi kweminyaka emihlanu. Amasu amasha namathuluzi Amazweasenze inqubekela phambili ekwandiseni ukungena kumisebenzi yamalaleveva kusibalo sabantu esisengcupheni kakhulu. Ngaphandle kokuqhubeka abantu abaningi abasengcupheni yamalaleveva basantula ukungena emisebenzini engavimba, noma itthole bese yelapha isifo. Izingqinamba ekwandeni kokungena kumisebenzi kamalaleveva sezihlanganisisiwe,ikakhulukazi esu-Saharan Afrika, ngobhubhane lweCOVID-19, ukujika izinkinga zobuntu, uxhasomali olunezivimbo, izinhlelo zokubheka ezintekenteke, ukwehla ekwenzeni kwamathuluzi okulwa ngqo nomalaleveva. Ukubhekana nalokhu okusabisayo nokweseka amazwe ekwakheni izinhlelo zokubekezela zamalaleveva, iWHO isanda kushicilela amasu amasha nomhlahlandlela: isu elisha lelinesidakamizwa esingenamalaleveva ukuvika eAfrika; umzamo omusha wokumisa ukusabalala kweAnophelesi stephensi ezindaweni zasemadolobheni;
translations.reference: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations. Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.hypothesis: According to the latest global malaria report, published in 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to bear the heaviest burden of the disease, accounting for 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all malaria deaths (593,000) in 2021. Malaria-related deaths are particularly high among children under five years of age, accounting for up to 80% of all deaths in the African region. However, new tools and strategies are being implemented to improve malaria control efforts. Many people in endemic areas are still not accessing effective prevention or treatment, due to a number of factors, including limited access to healthcare, lack of resources, and the emergence of drug-resistant malaria parasites. To address these challenges and strengthen malaria control programs, the WHO has recently released new guidelines: a new insecticide-treated net (ITN) distribution strategy for Africa; a new approach to controlling the Anopheles stephensi mosquito in urban areas;

translations.source: umhlahlandlela omusha, uthuthukiswe ndawonye yiWHO neUN-Habitat, ukuyalela babaholi bedolobha ekulawulweni kwemalalaeveva edolobheni. ithuluzi elisha ukusiza amazwe ahlola umalaleveva wabio wezinhlelo zokubheka nokuhlonza izindawo zotshalomali I-WHO iphinde yanyusa ukwethembeka, ukubalula nokuthola iziphakamiso zemalaleveva. Ukuhlanganiswa kwemiyalelo yeWHO kwamalaleveva sekukhona manje ngezinkundla ezimbili zedijithali: iMAGICapp kanye ne-"MalariaToolkit" isisetshenziswa. I-WHO igqugquzela amazwe ukuthi ahlanganise kuziphakamiso ekuhlelweni kwezifo zendawo kumthelela omkhulu. Izindlela zokungenelela okusha Ukuqhubeka kotshalomali ekuthuthukisweni nokubekwa kwemigomo emisha kamalaleveva namathuluzi esizukulwane esilandelayo kuzobalulekaukuthola okuqondwe ngomalaleveva emhlabeni 2030. Umgomo wesibili kamalaleveva, umgomo iR21/Matrix-M (R21), uma ugunyaziwe ungasiza ukuvala isikhala esikalekayo phakathi kwesaplayi mokufuneka nokuqhubeka ukwehlisa ukugula kwezingane nokufa okudalwa umalaleveva. Kuhamba phambili kuWHO ukuqhubeka ngokubheka kangoti kweR21 malaleveva ngesikhathi sekwengezwe okuphepha okubalulekile,nemininingo kusuka ekuqhubekeni kukaR21 isigaba 3 sesikhona nokinikezelwa kuWHO. Isigaba sokukhiqiza iRTS,S umgomo wamalaleveva emazweni engeziwe eAfrika kulindeleke ukuthi aqale ngokuqala kuka-2024.
translations.reference: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact. Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.hypothesis: The new guidelines, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, provide city leaders with guidance on malaria prevention and control within urban areas. The new tool helps countries to assess the risk of malaria transmission and to develop effective control strategies. The WHO has also strengthened its capacity to provide technical assistance and guidance on malaria. The integration of WHO malaria guidelines into national health plans is now a priority for many countries. New approaches to malaria control are being developed and implemented, including the use of new tools and technologies. The second malaria strategy, the R21/Matrix-M strategy, aims to eliminate malaria by 2030. This strategy focuses on a combination of interventions, including insecticide-treated nets, indoor residual spraying, and preventive chemotherapy. The WHO is working with countries to implement the R21/Matrix-M strategy. The RTS,S malaria vaccine is expected to be rolled out in several African countries starting in 2024.

translations.source: Endaweni yokulawula ivekitha,kukhona imikhiqizoemisha engu-28 kokuzayo kweR&D. Amathuluzi asabhekisiswa afka, isibonelo, izinhlobo ezintsha zamanethi okulapha izinambuzane, izicuphomziqonde ukudonsa omiyane, isipeshali ripheleli, okwasendlini, (i-eaves tubes) nobunjiniyela bofuzo lwemiyane. Abacwaningi bahambisa phambili ukuthuthuka kokulashwa okungeyona i-ACTS emikhakheni wemithi omele umalaleveva ngenxa yokuphuthuma nokusabalala kokungavuminokuncane kwe-artemisinin.
